content.text:
Here's what I see on the macOS desktop:

**System Permission Dialog** — A macOS permission prompt is displayed in the center of the screen. It reads:

> **"bash" is requesting to bypass the system private window picker and directly access your screen and audio.**
>
> This will allow bash to record your screen and system audio, including personal or sensitive information that may be visible or audible.

There are two buttons:
- **Allow** (blue, highlighted) — to grant the permission
- **Open System Settings** — to manage permissions manually

**Background/Desktop:**
- The desktop wallpaper is solid black
- The **menu bar** at the top shows: Apple logo, Finder menus (File, Edit, View, Go, Window, Help), and the time is **Tue Mar 3, 3:59 AM** in the top-right corner

**Dock** at the bottom contains the standard macOS app icons including: Finder, Launchpad, Safari, Messages, Mail, Maps, Photos, FaceTime, Calendar, Keka (file archiver), Reminders, Notes, Freeform, Apple TV, Music, News, App Store, System Settings, and Trash.
